testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Tune SQLite connection PRAGMAs for a local read-heavy app
userStories.overview: Both connection setups only set `journal_mode=WAL` (+ `foreign_keys` in src/lib). Best-practice PRAGMAs that materially reduce write latency and eliminate busy errors are missing.
acceptanceCriteria: All listed PRAGMAs set at both connection sites with a rationale comment.
Measured write/query improvement recorded; no busy errors under concurrent access.
`pnpm check` passes; relevant DB tests pass.
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
Both connection setups only set `journal_mode=WAL` (+ `foreign_keys` in src/lib). Best-practice PRAGMAs that materially reduce write latency and eliminate busy errors are missing.

## Evidence
`src/lib/db.ts:44-45`; `core/db.ts:1003,1020`.

## Fix — add after WAL at all connection sites
- `synchronous = NORMAL` — biggest write-latency win; still crash-safe under WAL.
- `busy_timeout = 5000` — avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` under concurrent UI + MCP + CLI access.
- `cache_size = -32000` (~32MB) — fewer page reads on board/analytics.
- `mmap_size = 268435456` (256MB) — memory-mapped reads.
- `temp_store = MEMORY` — keep GROUP BY/ORDER BY temp data off disk (analytics).

Apply at BOTH `src/lib/db.ts` and `core/db.ts` (CLI/MCP path). Verify values are sensible cross-platform.

## Hot paths
Every SQLite read/write; especially board load, analytics, status writes.

## Validation
Focused timing of a representative write (status update) and an analytics query before/after; confirm no `SQLITE_BUSY` under simultaneous UI + MCP access. Record numbers.

## Acceptance criteria
- All listed PRAGMAs set at both connection sites with a rationale comment.
- Measured write/query improvement recorded; no busy errors under concurrent access.
- `pnpm check` passes; relevant DB tests pass.

userStories.title: Add missing hot-path indexes: tickets.position and tickets.completed_at
userStories.overview:
Two hot-path queries sort/filter on unindexed columns:
- The board's `getTicketSummaries`/`getTickets` do `ORDER BY tickets.position` (`src/api/tickets.ts:444,98`) but `position` is not indexed — SQLite filesorts matching rows on every board load and reorder.
- Dashboard analytics filter/sort on `completed_at` in 5+ queries (`src/api/analytics.ts:88-96,123-130,248-252`) but `completed_at` is unindexed → repeated full scans.
acceptanceCriteria: Both indexes added to schema + migration generated/applied.
Query plans use the new indexes (no filesort on board ORDER BY position).
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
Two hot-path queries sort/filter on unindexed columns:
- The board's `getTicketSummaries`/`getTickets` do `ORDER BY tickets.position` (`src/api/tickets.ts:444,98`) but `position` is not indexed — SQLite filesorts matching rows on every board load and reorder.
- Dashboard analytics filter/sort on `completed_at` in 5+ queries (`src/api/analytics.ts:88-96,123-130,248-252`) but `completed_at` is unindexed → repeated full scans.

## Evidence
`src/lib/schema.ts:70-78` (existing ticket indexes; no `position`, no `completed_at`).

## Fix
- Add `index("idx_tickets_project_position").on(projectId, position)` (covers board filter+sort together).
- Add `index("idx_tickets_completed_at").on(completedAt)`.
- Generate + run the migration: `pnpm db:generate` then `pnpm db:migrate`. Also add to any imperative `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` init in `src/lib/db.ts` for existing DBs.

## Hot paths
Board load/reorder; dashboard analytics.

## Validation
`EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN` before/after shows index use instead of `SCAN ... USING TEMP B-TREE`; focused timing of the board summary + analytics queries. Record before/after.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both indexes added to schema + migration generated/applied.
- Query plans use the new indexes (no filesort on board ORDER BY position).
- `pnpm db:generate`/`pnpm db:migrate` clean; `pnpm check` passes.

userStories.title: Enable foreign_keys in core/db.ts (CLI/MCP path)
userStories.overview: `src/lib/db.ts` sets `foreign_keys = ON`, but `core/db.ts` (the connection used by the CLI and MCP server) sets only `journal_mode = WAL` — it does NOT enable foreign keys (`core/db.ts:1003-1004,1020-1021`). On the CLI/MCP path, cascades and FK constraints are silently unenforced, and the FK indexes go unused. This is primarily a correctness/reliability issue but also means inconsistent behavior between the web app and CLI/MCP writers.
acceptanceCriteria: `foreign_keys = ON` set at both `core/db.ts` connection sites.
CLI/MCP delete cascades behave consistently with the web app.
`pnpm check` and relevant core/MCP tests pass.
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
`src/lib/db.ts` sets `foreign_keys = ON`, but `core/db.ts` (the connection used by the CLI and MCP server) sets only `journal_mode = WAL` — it does NOT enable foreign keys (`core/db.ts:1003-1004,1020-1021`). On the CLI/MCP path, cascades and FK constraints are silently unenforced, and the FK indexes go unused. This is primarily a correctness/reliability issue but also means inconsistent behavior between the web app and CLI/MCP writers.

## Fix
- Add `db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON")` at both `core/db.ts` connection sites, matching `src/lib/db.ts`.
- Verify existing CLI/MCP tests still pass (some may have relied on cascades NOT firing — fix those if they were masking a bug).

## Evidence
`core/db.ts:1003-1004,1020-1021` vs `src/lib/db.ts:45`.

## Hot paths
MCP tool dispatch, CLI operations (writes/deletes).

## Validation
Confirm a cascade-dependent delete behaves identically via CLI/MCP and the web app. Run core + MCP tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- `foreign_keys = ON` set at both `core/db.ts` connection sites.
- CLI/MCP delete cascades behave consistently with the web app.
- `pnpm check` and relevant core/MCP tests pass.

userStories.title: Use summary column projection for board/list (stop getTickets SELECT *)
userStories.overview: `getTickets` does `db.select().from(tickets)` — a full `SELECT *` returning `description`, `linked_files`, `attachments`, `subtasks` (potentially large JSON blobs) for every ticket (`src/api/tickets.ts:98`). Board/list views only need summary fields, and a `getTicketSummaries` endpoint with a `TICKET_SUMMARY_COLUMNS` projection already exists (`src/api/tickets.ts:401-421,444`). Using the full-row endpoint for summary views wastes serialization + transfer on every board render and status change.
acceptanceCriteria: Board/list summary views use the projected `getTicketSummaries` (no `SELECT *`).
Detail views still get full data.
Measured payload/time reduction recorded; `pnpm check` passes.
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
`getTickets` does `db.select().from(tickets)` — a full `SELECT *` returning `description`, `linked_files`, `attachments`, `subtasks` (potentially large JSON blobs) for every ticket (`src/api/tickets.ts:98`). Board/list views only need summary fields, and a `getTicketSummaries` endpoint with a `TICKET_SUMMARY_COLUMNS` projection already exists (`src/api/tickets.ts:401-421,444`). Using the full-row endpoint for summary views wastes serialization + transfer on every board render and status change.

## Fix
- Audit UI call sites of `getTickets`; migrate board/list/summary consumers to `getTicketSummaries` (projected columns).
- Keep `getTickets`/full-row fetch only for detail surfaces that actually need description/attachments/subtasks.
- Ensure query keys/types line up so components get the right shape.

## Evidence
`src/api/tickets.ts:98,401-421,444`.

## Hot paths
Board/list load and re-render after status change.

## Validation
Measure payload size + query time for the board data path before/after. Confirm board/list render identically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Board/list summary views use the projected `getTicketSummaries` (no `SELECT *`).
- Detail views still get full data.
- Measured payload/time reduction recorded; `pnpm check` passes.

userStories.title: Eliminate N+1 query loops in cost computation paths
userStories.overview:
Several cost paths issue a query per row inside a loop:
- `getCostExplorerData` calls `getTicketCostDetail(db, row.ticket_id)` per ticket; each does ~4 queries → ~`1 + 4N` queries (`core/cost.ts:1502-1503,1584-1585,1610-1611`).
- `recalculateCosts` calls `computeCostFromTokens` per `token_usage` row, and each does 2 `cost_models` lookups → ~`2 * rows` queries inside the transaction (`core/cost.ts:1141-1175`).
- `sessionHasUsage` runs a `COUNT(*)` per session in the deep recalculate backfills (`core/deep-cost-recalculate.ts:202,351,522`).
acceptanceCriteria: Cost explorer, recalculate, and deep-recalculate no longer issue per-row/per-session queries.
Cost numbers unchanged (verified against current output).
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
Several cost paths issue a query per row inside a loop:
- `getCostExplorerData` calls `getTicketCostDetail(db, row.ticket_id)` per ticket; each does ~4 queries → ~`1 + 4N` queries (`core/cost.ts:1502-1503,1584-1585,1610-1611`).
- `recalculateCosts` calls `computeCostFromTokens` per `token_usage` row, and each does 2 `cost_models` lookups → ~`2 * rows` queries inside the transaction (`core/cost.ts:1141-1175`).
- `sessionHasUsage` runs a `COUNT(*)` per session in the deep recalculate backfills (`core/deep-cost-recalculate.ts:202,351,522`).

## Fix
- `getCostExplorerData`: pre-fetch ralph_sessions + token_usage aggregates for ALL ticket IDs in one query each, then join in memory in `buildTicketNode`.
- `recalculateCosts`: load `cost_models` into a `Map` once before the loop; resolve pricing in memory (no per-row DB query).
- Deep recalculate: pre-fetch the set of session IDs that already have usage via one `GROUP BY`/`IN (...)` query → build a Set.

## Hot paths
Cost Explorer page; cost recalculate / backfill commands.

## Validation
Count queries (or time) for cost explorer on an epic with ~20 tickets before/after; recalculate on a large token_usage set. Record before/after.

## Acceptance criteria
- Cost explorer, recalculate, and deep-recalculate no longer issue per-row/per-session queries.
- Cost numbers unchanged (verified against current output).
- `pnpm check` and cost tests pass.

userStories.title: Cheapen startup & search DB checks (user_version gating, FTS cache, integrity_check(1))
userStories.overview: Startup runs ~25 synchronous `sqlite_master`/`PRAGMA table_info` checks on every boot (`src/lib/db.ts:82-758`, `src/lib/db-bootstrap.ts:3-25`), adding ~10-50ms to first-request latency even when no migration is needed. Two smaller repeat-cost checks: `hasFts5Table` queries `sqlite_master` on every search keystroke (`core/search.ts:33-38`), and `verifyBackup` uses full `integrity_check` instead of the cheaper `integrity_check(1)` (`src/lib/backup.ts:179`).
acceptanceCriteria: Migration checks skipped via `user_version` when DB is current.
No migration/correctness regression; `pnpm check` + DB/backup tests pass.
userStories.description:
> 🌿 **Branch:** Implement on the existing `chore/project-validation-discovery` branch — do NOT create a per-ticket branch. **Planning only:** do not start work until explicitly requested.

## Problem
Startup runs ~25 synchronous `sqlite_master`/`PRAGMA table_info` checks on every boot (`src/lib/db.ts:82-758`, `src/lib/db-bootstrap.ts:3-25`), adding ~10-50ms to first-request latency even when no migration is needed. Two smaller repeat-cost checks: `hasFts5Table` queries `sqlite_master` on every search keystroke (`core/search.ts:33-38`), and `verifyBackup` uses full `integrity_check` instead of the cheaper `integrity_check(1)` (`src/lib/backup.ts:179`).

## Fix
- Gate the migration/column checks behind `PRAGMA user_version`: bump `user_version` after migrations; skip all checks when it matches the current version (standard SQLite migration pattern).
- Cache the `hasFts5Table` boolean (module-level / per-DB WeakMap) — it never changes at runtime.
- Change `verifyBackup` to `integrity_check(1)` (stop at first error), matching the startup path.
- Where multiple `PRAGMA table_info(<table>)` calls hit the same table, fetch once and reuse.

## Hot paths
App/server startup; search keystrokes; backup creation/restore.

## Validation
Time cold startup (first-request DB init) before/after with an already-migrated DB; confirm search and backup verify still correct. Record before/after.

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration checks skipped via `user_version` when DB is current.
- `hasFts5Table` cached; `verifyBackup` uses `integrity_check(1)`.
- No migration/correctness regression; `pnpm check` + DB/backup tests pass.

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: ⚡ SQLite Data Layer Speed (PRAGMAs, indexes, N+1)
epicDescription:
## Why this epic exists

Every UI interaction ultimately reads/writes local SQLite. If the data layer isn't tuned, the whole app inherits avoidable latency. Investigation found missing connection PRAGMAs, missing indexes on hot-path sort/filter columns, several N+1 query loops, and over-fetching on the board.

## Root causes found (with evidence)

- **Missing PRAGMAs** (`src/lib/db.ts:44-45`, `core/db.ts:1003,1020`): only `journal_mode=WAL` + `foreign_keys` are set. Missing `synchronous=NORMAL` (biggest write-latency win), `busy_timeout` (causes SQLITE_BUSY under UI+MCP+CLI contention), `cache_size`, `mmap_size`, `temp_store=MEMORY`.
- **`core/db.ts` does not set `foreign_keys=ON`** — CLI/MCP path silently bypasses cascades.
- **Missing index on `tickets.position`** — the board's `ORDER BY position` falls back to filesort on every load/reorder (`src/lib/schema.ts:70-78`, `src/api/tickets.ts:444`).
- **Missing index on `tickets.completed_at`** — dashboard analytics does 5+ full scans (`src/api/analytics.ts:88-130,248`).
- **N+1 queries**: `getCostExplorerData` ~4 queries/ticket (`core/cost.ts:1502,1584,1610`); `recalculateCosts` 2 cost-model queries/row (`core/cost.ts:1166`); `sessionHasUsage` per session (`core/deep-cost-recalculate.ts`).
- **Over-fetch**: `getTickets` does `SELECT *` (description/attachments/subtasks JSON) for board views instead of the existing `getTicketSummaries` projection (`src/api/tickets.ts:98`).
- **Startup**: ~25 uncached `PRAGMA table_info`/`sqlite_master` checks run every boot; could be gated behind `PRAGMA user_version`. `hasFts5Table` queries `sqlite_master` on every search keystroke. `verifyBackup` uses full `integrity_check` instead of `integrity_check(1)`.

## Goal

Connection tuned for a local read-heavy app, hot-path queries index-backed, no N+1 loops, board fetches only summary columns, startup checks cheap. Prove with focused query timing.

## 🌿 BRANCH REQUIREMENT

All tickets in this epic MUST be implemented on the existing branch **`chore/project-validation-discovery`** (no per-ticket branches). PLANNING ONLY — do not start work until requested. DB schema changes require `pnpm db:generate`/`pnpm db:migrate` per CLAUDE.md. See the ⚡ Performance North Star epic for validation methodology.
